forward_patch: --- original +++ modified @@ -0,0 +1,67 @@ +--- +assignees: +- claude-code +position_column: todo +position_ordinal: ff9780 +title: Normalize compact field-display heights for virtualized grid rows +--- +## What + +Visual regression discovered while testing the `DataTable` row-virtualization work (task `01KQ1XWCWHT7C4T71T02V99AGP`). The virtualizer assumes a fixed `ROW_HEIGHT = 32px` per row, but compact-mode field displays render at different heights depending on whether the cell is populated or empty. With the virtualizer reserving exactly 32px per row, taller populated cells overflow their reserved slot â€” causing rows to render at visibly inconsistent heights and the absolute scroll position to drift from the actual rendered positions. + +Two confirmed offenders, both surfaced by the user: + +1. **`tags` field** (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/badge-list-display.tsx`) â€” empty state renders `{field.placeholder ?? "-"}` (~16-18px content height); populated state renders CM6-rendered `MentionView` pills which are visibly taller. The YAML `placeholder: "Add tags"` is wired up correctly â€” the issue is purely the height mismatch between the plain `` and the CM6 widget pipeline. + +2. **`assignees` field** (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/avatar-display.tsx`): + - **Bug A**: hardcodes `-` for the empty state and **ignores `field.placeholder` entirely** â€” unlike `BadgeListDisplay` and `BadgeDisplay` which honor it. Inconsistent with the established placeholder convention. + - **Bug B**: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/definitions/assignees.yaml` has no `placeholder` field â€” needs one added (e.g. `placeholder: "Assign"`). + - **Bug C**: populated state renders `` which is `w-7 h-7` = 28px (see `kanban-app/ui/src/components/avatar.tsx:24`). Plus row `py-1.5` padding (6px Ă— 2 = 12px), the populated cell is ~40px tall, while the empty `` is ~18px. Hence the height divergence. + +The right fix is to normalize compact-mode display heights at the display-component level so populated and empty variants render the same height. This is correct independent of virtualization (the variable heights would have caused subtle row-jitter before too â€” virtualization just made it loud), and it preserves the perf win of fixed-height virtualization without falling back to `measureElement`. + +**Files:** +- Modify: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/avatar-display.tsx` â€” add `field` to props (matching `DisplayProps`), use `field.placeholder` in the empty-state branch (mirror `BadgeListDisplay`'s `EmptyBadgeList` pattern), and ensure both populated and empty variants share a fixed compact-mode height. +- Modify: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/definitions/assignees.yaml` â€” add `placeholder: "Assign"` (or whichever string the team prefers â€” match the imperative tone of `"Add tags"`). +- Modify: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/badge-list-display.tsx` â€” wrap `EmptyBadgeList`'s compact branch (and probably the `MentionView` branch too, though `MentionView` may already be height-stable) so both render at the same fixed compact height. +- Audit (read-only): every other display in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/*.tsx` â€” `text-display`, `badge-display`, `date-display`, `markdown-display`, `number-display`, `progress-display`, `progress-ring-display`, `status-date-display`, `virtual-tag-display`, `attachment-display`, `color-swatch-display`. For each, verify the populated and empty variants render at the same height in `mode="compact"`. Fix any that diverge. +- Verify: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.tsx` `ROW_HEIGHT` constant matches the normalized cell-content height + `py-1.5` padding. Adjust the constant if the normalized height changes the math. + +**Approach:** + +1. Pick a target compact-mode content height. The smallest currently-wired compact display sets the floor â€” `Avatar size="md"` at 28px is the largest non-CM6 element. A 20â€“24px content height (text-line + small badge) is a sensible target; verify by measuring the actual rendered row in dev. Encode it as a shared Tailwind class (e.g. `h-6 flex items-center` on every compact-mode wrapper) or a shared `CompactCellWrapper` component. Prefer the wrapper component if more than two displays need the treatment â€” DRY beats copy-paste. +2. For `AvatarDisplay`: shrink to `size="sm"` (`w-5 h-5` = 20px) when in `mode="compact"` â€” the existing `md` size is too tall for grid cells anyway. Match the height with the empty ``. +3. For `BadgeListDisplay`: `MentionView` in compact mode already renders inline pills; verify their height. If they exceed the target, constrain via the wrapper. +4. For `AvatarDisplay`'s placeholder: add `field` to `AvatarDisplayProps`, then `{field.placeholder ?? "-"}` mirrors `BadgeListDisplay`'s pattern. Also pass `mode` so the empty-state can use `text-sm italic` for `mode="full"` like `EmptyBadgeList` does. +5. Update `assignees.yaml` with `placeholder: "Assign"`. Verify the schema reload picks it up (no Rust changes needed â€” it's a string field on `FieldDef` already). +6. After the displays are normalized, re-measure `ROW_HEIGHT` against a real running grid (`cd kanban-app && bun tauri dev`) and adjust the constant in `data-table.tsx` if needed. + +**Out of scope:** +- Switching the virtualizer to dynamic `measureElement` â€” fixed-height virtualization is the right tradeoff once content heights are uniform. +- Refactoring the display registration system or the `DisplayProps` interface beyond adding what's missing. +- Touching the editor variants (these only render in `mode="edit"` which isn't subject to row-height invariants the same way). + +## Acceptance Criteria + +- [ ] `assignees.yaml` declares `placeholder: "Assign"` (or agreed string). +- [ ] `AvatarDisplay` reads `field.placeholder` and renders it in the empty-state span (with `-` as the legacy fallback) â€” matching the convention in `BadgeListDisplay`/`BadgeDisplay`. +- [ ] `AvatarDisplay` accepts `mode` and uses `text-sm italic` styling for the `mode="full"` empty state, mirroring `EmptyBadgeList`. +- [ ] In a grid view, every row is the **exact same pixel height** regardless of which fields are populated, empty, or contain tags/assignees. No visible jitter when scrolling. Verify in dev: `cd kanban-app && bun tauri dev`. +- [ ] In `mode="compact"`, the rendered height of every display under `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/` is identical for both populated and empty value states. +- [ ] `ROW_HEIGHT` in `data-table.tsx` matches the actual rendered row height â€” no row content overflows or under-fills its reserved virtualizer slot. +- [ ] No new console warnings/errors during initial mount or scroll. + +## Tests + +- [ ] Add a test in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/avatar-display.test.tsx` (create the file if needed) asserting that with `field.placeholder = "Assign"` and `value = []`, the empty-state span renders the placeholder text â€” not `-`. Also assert that with `value = []` and no placeholder, `-` is the fallback (regression guard). +- [ ] Extend `data-table.virtualized.test.tsx` (added by the virt task): render two `DataTable`s â€” one with all rows populated, one with all rows empty â€” and assert the rendered `` heights are equal. This catches future displays that break the invariant. +- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && bun run test fields/displays` â€” all display tests still pass. +- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && bun run test data-table` â€” virtualization tests still pass with the (possibly updated) `ROW_HEIGHT`. +- [ ] Run full UI suite: `cd kanban-app/ui && bun run test` â€” no new failures, no new warnings. +- [ ] Manual smoke: `cd kanban-app && bun tauri dev`, open the task grid view (rows have varying tag/assignee populations), scroll. All rows render at identical height with no jitter. + +## Workflow + +- Use `/tdd` â€” write the AvatarDisplay placeholder test first (RED), wire up `field.placeholder` (GREEN), then move to the height-normalization audit. Add the height-equality test in `data-table.virtualized.test.tsx` before normalizing, so it fails with the current state and passes after the fix. + +#performance #frontend #kanban-app \ No newline at end of file
